task: I’m trying to plan for a class or meeting that could get interrupted by a power or tech issue, but I still need people to leave with the key info. Can you help me sketch a simple backup approach that would still work if the usual setup falls apart?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a simple backup approach you can use if power or tech fails, while still making sure people leave with the key information.

## Simple backup plan

### 1) Decide the “must-have” outcome
Pick the 3–5 most important things people need to know or do by the end.  
If everything else fails, those are the essentials to cover.

### 2) Prepare a cut-down version of the session
Make a very short version you can run without slides, projector, or internet:
- 1-minute welcome
- 3 main points
- 1 example or demo if possible
- 1 final takeaway or next step
- Q&A if time allows

### 3) Bring a paper backup
Have printed copies of:
- agenda or outline
- handout with key info
- sign-in sheet
- any instructions or next steps

### 4) Save files in more than one place
Keep the important files:
- on your laptop
- on your phone
- on a USB drive
- optionally in cloud storage if available

### 5) Have a simple contact plan
If the room setup or power fails, know who to contact first for help:
1. room or building support
2. space owner or event contact
3. host or organizer

### 6) Use a fallback way to share info
If the screen or audio stops working:
- talk through the key points
- hand out printed materials
- write the main points on a whiteboard or flip chart
- collect contact info so you can send follow-up materials later

### 7) Keep a short “if everything breaks” script
Example:
> “We’ve had a tech issue, so I’m going to switch to the backup plan. I’ll cover the key points verbally and with printed handouts so you still leave with the main information.”

If you want, I can turn this into a one-page, print-ready backup plan with a checklist and a short script.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, this is solid. Simple and usable. The only thing I’d add is to make sure the printed handouts match the exact key points, not just a general outline, because I’ve had it where a file didn’t save right and then I was stuck rebuilding stuff from notes. Also worth having one person’s phone number for building support written down, not just in your contacts.

So basically: key points, printed backup, offline version, and a short script. That covers most of the mess if the power goes out or the system acts up.
